title: Define Strategy for Scheduled vs On-Demand Data Refresh
description:
Define the strategy for data refresh in DataTools Pro: scheduled background refresh (cron jobs) versus on-demand end-user-driven refresh. This is a discovery and alignment task to determine which jobs can technically run without an end user being logged in, and which should remain user-triggered.

The outcome should document the chosen strategy and update relevant documentation including the product README and tool-specific docs where they reference sync behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Strategy for scheduled vs on-demand refresh is chosen and documented
docs/product/README.md is updated with the refresh strategy
Tool-specific documentation is updated where they reference sync behavior
Clear definition of which jobs can run autonomously vs which require user session
middleware_changes: Strategy decision for cron-based scheduled jobs vs on-demand user-triggered refresh. Implementation will follow based on chosen approach.
implementation_notes: Part of Phase 4. Current options include on-end-user refresh. Need to assess which jobs can run headlessly via cron and which require an active user session. See docs/JIRA_TICKET_BACKLOG.md Phase 4.
